Common.portfolioNote: All records are fictional sample data created for portfolio presentation.

Landing.title: Run a fabric business from one clean control center.
Landing.description: FabricLog presents a polished full-stack demo for textile operations: customers, stock, orders, invoices, and payment follow-up inside a recruiter-friendly SaaS experience.

highlights.customers: Customer portfolio, regional accounts, and collection visibility
highlights.inventory: Fabric catalog with stock pressure, reservations, and pricing
highlights.orders: Order pipeline from sampling to dispatch with delivery targets
highlights.finance: Invoice and payment tracking with realistic summaries

Landing.safetyDescription: No secrets, no production services, no personal data. Every company, contact, order, and transaction in this demo is fictional.

Dashboard.title: Dashboard
Dashboard.description: Monitor sales momentum, low stock pressure, invoice exposure, and payment collection performance.

charts.revenueDescription: Monthly booked revenue and invoice volume

Dashboard.insightsDescription: Quick actions and business notes based on current mock performance.

Customers.title: Customers
Customers.description: Keep high-value accounts, outstanding balances, and preferred collections visible in one place.

Fabrics.title: Fabrics
Fabrics.description: Track popular textiles, stock position, and reservation pressure across the catalog.

Products.title: Products
Products.description: Present the textile catalog as a polished product library with stock, reservations, and pricing visibility.

Orders.title: Orders
Orders.description: Review the live order book from new requests through production and dispatch.

form.helper: Illustrative React Hook Form + Zod setup for future order create/edit flows.

Invoices.title: Invoices
Invoices.description: Stay ahead of due dates, overdue exposure, and partial collections.

Payments.title: Payments
Payments.description: Track payment timing, methods, and customers that need follow-up.

notes.primary: The payment ledger mixes bank transfers, wire receipts, and card settlements to make the demo feel operationally realistic without using any private billing information.
notes.secondary: Portfolio viewers can inspect both the UI and the typed API route layer to see how payment status tracking is modeled end to end.

Reports.title: Reports
Reports.description: Preview the reporting layer for sales, collections, customers, and product performance in a presentation-ready analytics hub.

panels.collectionsDescription: Monitor where reporting will highlight overdue exposure and partial settlements.
panels.collectionsBody: This section is reserved for monthly receivables packs, customer collection trends, and invoice aging snapshots.
panels.catalogDescription: Track which references, categories, and reserved lines are pulling the most demand.
panels.catalogBody: Future reports will compare stock pressure, reservation ratios, and price movement across the portfolio.
panels.roadmapDescription: The shell is ready for richer charting and export workflows.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.description: Show how FabricLog can grow into a configurable workspace for branding, operations, localization, and governance.

panels.profileDescription: Public demo metadata for the fictional FabricLog workspace.
panels.profileBody: Use this area later for company info, tax preferences, invoice templates, and order reference rules.
panels.experienceDescription: Prepare the app for language, date, and dashboard preference management.
panels.experienceBody: The current structure already supports English and Azerbaijani and can grow into user-level preferences without a layout rewrite.
panels.controlsDescription: Future-ready placeholders for permissions, exports, and integrations.

primary.title: Priority collection window
primary.description: Two partial invoices are due within the next five business days. A quick follow-up keeps the monthly collection rate above target.

secondary.title: Reserve planning
secondary.description: Premium jacquard and linen references are seeing the heaviest reservation pressure. Replenishment should focus there first.
